Author: Maximilian Oliver
Title: How I Use AI to Code Faster Than I Thought Possible (Without Losing My Sanity)
Text:
Member-only story
How I Use AI to Code Faster Than I Thought Possible (Without Losing My Sanity)
Real Stories and Tactics from a Developer Who Uses Large Language Models Daily — Not Just for Fun, but for Real Delivery.
AI isn’t just changing how we code — it’s changing who gets to code fast.
In this article, I’m sharing my real-world, day-to-day experiences using AI tools like GPT-4, GitHub Copilot, and Cursor to reduce dev cycles from hours to minutes. No hype. No marketing fluff. Just the strategies that helped me ship faster, debug smarter, and focus deeper.
1. How I Started Using LLMs in My Dev Workflow
I first started using AI tools out of curiosity. It started small — asking ChatGPT to generate a regex here or a quick Python snippet there.
Now, AI is woven into every part of my workflow.
I don’t treat ChatGPT or Copilot like magic — I treat them like junior devs who never sleep, and need clear direction. The better I prompt, the better they perform.
2. From Blank Page to Working Prototype in Minutes
The most brutal part of software development? Starting.
Before:
I’d stare at a blank editor wondering how to architect a tool, write a test suite, or integrate an API.
Now:
I feed ChatGPT my intent + high-level plan, and it gives me a full draft.
